spec_match.rationale: Exercises the exact spec syntax: polygon([nonzero|evenodd]? round <length>? <points>). The matrix of {nonzero, evenodd} × {sharp, rounded} on a self-intersecting star is precisely the headline interaction this feature unlocks.

interactive.rationale: Tab buttons for fill-rule, radius slider, shape selector, live code readout, and a 2x2 matrix that updates in real time. Well past static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises every combination across self-intersecting shapes — page delivers a matrix and explainer.

edge_cases.rationale: Star is a good self-intersecting case but no non-self-intersecting polygon (where fill-rule has no observable difference) — that null result is itself spec-worthy. No demo of nested holes (pentagram-in-pentagon) where evenodd matters most.

references.rationale: see-also section does NOT include the chromestatus.com/feature/6636392944893952 link directly (grep returned 0). Per CLAUDE.md rule 3 every page must include it for the self-heal recovery script.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, fill-rule tab buttons override background AND color together, matrix cells use --bg-paper / --bg-stone properly.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus.com link in see-also
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md mandates every page link the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> URL for the slug self-heal recovery. This page has see-also without that link. Add it (and the MDN polygon() link sibling concepts use).

openQuestions.title: Add a non-self-intersecting case
openQuestions.detail: Show that fill-rule has no observable effect on a triangle or convex pentagon — the null result is part of the spec story and teaches authors when to bother specifying it.

openQuestions.title: Add a pentagram-in-pentagon nested case
openQuestions.detail: The canonical evenodd demo is a pentagram inscribed in a pentagon where the inner star is 'hollow'. This page uses only a 10-point star — fine for crossings, but the canonical hole-punching case is missing.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the spec's argument order
openQuestions.detail: The spec says fill-rule must come before round in the function. Add a deliberately-malformed example (polygon(round 12px evenodd, ...)) and show it fails to parse with a code annotation — teaching the argument-order constraint.

summary: Best spec-syntax demonstration in the polygon cluster — the 2x2 matrix of fill-rule × radius is exactly the new combination 150 enables. Missing the chromestatus reference link entirely (a CLAUDE.md rule violation) and the null/non-self-intersecting case.
